TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Kanakanavu, story: kkvNr-story_pani
TEXT.citation: Sung, L. M., Lily, I., Hsieh, F., & Lin, Z. (2008). Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages. Taiwan Journal of Linguistics, 6(2.).
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @article{sung2008developing,title={Developing an online corpus of Formosan languages.},author={Sung, Li-May and Lily, I and Hsieh, Fuhui and Lin, Zhemin and others},journal={Taiwan Journal of Linguistics},volume={6},number={2},year={2008}}

S.FORM: teku poisua niaranaikita Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: teku poisua niar~ɾanaikita kanakanavu.
S.FORM: teku poisua niaranaikita Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: teku poisua niar~ɾanaikita kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 我要講關於我們Kanakanavu是從哪裡來的.
I am talking about where we Kanakanavu are from.

S.FORM: eh sua tinimanamaku 'una mamarang makasi ikita Kanakanavu miana nimon kankita racʉnga misee.
S.PHON: eh sua tinimanamaku ʔuna mamar~ɾaŋ makasi ikita kanakanavu miana nimon kankita r~ɾaʦɨŋa misee.
S.FORM: eh sua tinimanamaku 'una mamarang makasi ikita Kanakanavu miana nimon kankita racʉnga misee.
S.PHON: eh sua tinimanamaku ʔuna mamar~ɾaŋ makasi ikita kanakanavu miana nimon kankita r~ɾaʦɨŋa misee.
S.TRANSL: 有老人家說 我們卡族曾經住在Racʉnga這個地方.
According to the old sayings, once we were living in Racʉnga.

S.FORM: nakai kisonenmaku mamarang 'esi nanu sua racʉnga sua kari mamarang ia makasi maa ikua.
S.PHON: nakai kisonenmaku mamar~ɾaŋ ʔesi nanu sua r~ɾaʦɨŋa sua kar~ɾi mamar~ɾaŋ ia makasi maa ikua.
S.FORM: nakai kisonenmaku mamarang 'esi nanu sua racʉnga sua kari mamarang ia makasi maa ikua.
S.PHON: nakai kisonenmaku mamar~ɾaŋ ʔesi nanu sua r~ɾaʦɨŋa sua kar~ɾi mamar~ɾaŋ ia makasi maa ikua.
S.TRANSL: 但是我請教長輩們Racʉnga在哪裡,老人家只這樣回答我.
But, when I asked our elders where Racʉnga was, they just answered me like this.

S.FORM: cʉroo tanairʉ sua tararakaua taniarʉ ia isua to'onaa racʉnga misee.
S.PHON: ʦɨr~ɾoo tanair~ɾɨ sua tar~ɾar~ɾakaua taniar~ɾɨ ia isua toʔonaa r~ɾaʦɨŋa misee.
S.FORM: cʉroo tanairʉ sua tararakaua taniarʉ ia isua to'onaa racʉnga misee.
S.PHON: ʦɨr~ɾoo tanair~ɾɨ sua tar~ɾar~ɾakaua taniar~ɾɨ ia isua toʔonaa r~ɾaʦɨŋa misee.
S.TRANSL: 你看著太陽,太陽出現的地方,那裡就是Racʉnga的地方.
Look, where the sun arises is the place where Racʉnga was.

S.FORM: ka'an tavar'ʉ'ʉ ʉngcoo tutuuru tumatuuturu sua 'esi nanu sua Racʉnga.
S.PHON: kaʔan tavar~ɾʔɨʔɨ ɨŋʦoo tutuur~ɾu tumatuutur~ɾu sua ʔesi nanu sua r~ɾaʦɨŋa.
S.FORM: ka'an tavar'ʉ'ʉ ʉngcoo tutuuru tumatuuturu sua 'esi nanu sua Racʉnga.
S.PHON: kaʔan tavar~ɾʔɨʔɨ ɨŋʦoo tutuur~ɾu tumatuutur~ɾu sua ʔesi nanu sua r~ɾaʦɨŋa.
S.TRANSL: 沒有辦法明確說出來Racʉnga究竟在哪個位子.
He could not give me a clear answer about where Racʉnga was.

S.FORM: timanaku kangvang 'una Ra'arua nguani misee sua to'onaa nguani miana ia 'esi kani hlacʉna.
S.PHON: timanaku kaŋvaŋ ʔuna r~ɾaʔar~ɾua ŋuani misee sua toʔonaa ŋuani miana ia ʔesi kani h*aʦɨna.
S.FORM: timanaku kangvang 'una Ra'arua nguani misee sua to'onaa nguani miana ia 'esi kani hlacʉna.
S.PHON: timanaku kaŋvaŋ ʔuna r~ɾaʔar~ɾua ŋuani misee sua toʔonaa ŋuani miana ia ʔesi kani h*aʦɨna.
S.TRANSL: 我也曾經聽過拉阿魯阿族的人說他們以前所住的地方叫就Hlacʉna.
I also heard that Ra'arua had once lived in a place called Hlacʉna.

S.FORM: 'esiku pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉ hlcʉna mataa nacʉnga matʉakara 'ʉnai isi.
S.PHON: ʔesiku paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨ h*ʦɨna mataa naʦɨŋa matɨakar~ɾa ʔɨnai isi.
S.FORM: 'esiku pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉ hlcʉna mataa nacʉnga matʉakara 'ʉnai isi.
S.PHON: ʔesiku paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨ h*ʦɨna mataa naʦɨŋa matɨakar~ɾa ʔɨnai isi.
S.TRANSL: 我在思索著hlcʉna跟nacʉnga這兩個地名是同一個的地方嗎?
I am thinking if the two locations mean the same place.

S.FORM: sua isi ia pacʉpʉngʉcumaku masini.
S.PHON: sua isi ia paʦɨpɨŋɨʦumaku masini.
S.FORM: sua isi ia pacʉpʉngʉcumaku masini.
S.PHON: sua isi ia paʦɨpɨŋɨʦumaku masini.
S.TRANSL: 這些到現在我還在思索.
Until now, I am still thinking about this question.

S.FORM: rancurunga ia 'esi na masini to'onaamita.
S.PHON: r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa ia ʔesi na masini toʔonaamita.
S.FORM: rancurunga ia 'esi na masini to'onaamita.
S.PHON: r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa ia ʔesi na masini toʔonaamita.
S.TRANSL: Rancurunga這個地方在現在我們所住的地方.
Rancurunga is located at the place where we lived now.

S.FORM: ʉncoo uh uh 'esi na masini Namasia.
S.PHON: ɨnʦoo uh uh ʔesi na masini namasia.
S.FORM: ʉncoo uh uh 'esi na masini Namasia.
S.PHON: ɨnʦoo uh uh ʔesi na masini namasia.
S.TRANSL: 現在那瑪夏區的…
It is now at…

S.FORM: ʉncoo amuseni kʉcʉ 'ʉnai isua?
S.PHON: ɨnʦoo amuseni kɨʦɨ ʔɨnai isua?
S.FORM: ʉncoo amuseni kʉcʉ 'ʉnai isua?
S.PHON: ɨnʦoo amuseni kɨʦɨ ʔɨnai isua?
S.TRANSL: 那個地名究竟怎麼稱呼去了?
What is the name of that place?

S.FORM: uh ah! 'esi na nangisarʉ tamna cimʉrʉ.
S.PHON: uh ah! ʔesi na naŋisar~ɾɨ tamna ʦimɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: uh ah! 'esi na nangisarʉ tamna cimʉrʉ.
S.PHON: uh ah! ʔesi na naŋisar~ɾɨ tamna ʦimɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 那個位置在屬於南沙魯里的山上.
It is located at the mountain of Nangisarʉ.

S.FORM: 'una 'ʉnai taca cacaruin ia tʉnʉkaiin ia 'una 'ʉnai rancurunga kisʉʉnmita Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: ʔuna ʔɨnai taʦa ʦaʦar~ɾuin ia tɨnɨkaiin ia ʔuna ʔɨnai r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa kisɨɨnmita kanakanavu.
S.FORM: 'una 'ʉnai taca cacaruin ia tʉnʉkaiin ia 'una 'ʉnai rancurunga kisʉʉnmita Kanakanavu.
S.PHON: ʔuna ʔɨnai taʦa ʦaʦar~ɾuin ia tɨnɨkaiin ia ʔuna ʔɨnai r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa kisɨɨnmita kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 在他的頂端有一個地方卡那卡那富稱其為Rangurunga.
At the crown layer of the trees, there is a place we call Rangurunga.

S.FORM: nimoon 'inia sasarunai Kanakanvu 'uma'anupu aracakanʉ 'inia.
S.PHON: nimoon ʔinia sasar~ɾunai kanakanvu ʔumaʔanupu ar~ɾaʦakanɨ ʔinia.
S.FORM: nimoon 'inia sasarunai Kanakanvu 'uma'anupu aracakanʉ 'inia.
S.PHON: nimoon ʔinia sasar~ɾunai kanakanvu ʔumaʔanupu ar~ɾaʦakanɨ ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 一個卡那卡那富男子帶著他的狗打獵.
There was a Kanakanavu male hunting with his dog.

S.FORM: sua nericeni tacau ia 'una kan nipivaru.
S.PHON: sua ner~ɾiʦeni taʦau ia ʔuna kan nipivar~ɾu.
S.FORM: sua nericeni tacau ia 'una kan nipivaru.
S.PHON: sua ner~ɾiʦeni taʦau ia ʔuna kan nipivar~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 據說他所帶的狗有懷孕的.
There were dogs who were having babies.

S.FORM: 'una nikamanumanu tacau.
S.PHON: ʔuna nikamanumanu taʦau.
S.FORM: 'una nikamanumanu tacau.
S.PHON: ʔuna nikamanumanu taʦau.
S.TRANSL: 有生出小狗的母狗.
There were dogs who have given birth.

S.FORM: mia turupangcu nguani aracakanʉ 'inia ia nevici nguani tacau isia isua pue'i na Racʉnga.
S.PHON: mia tur~ɾupaŋʦu ŋuani ar~ɾaʦakanɨ ʔinia ia neviʦi ŋuani taʦau isia isua pueʔi na r~ɾaʦɨŋa.
S.FORM: mia turupangcu nguani aracakanʉ 'inia ia nevici nguani tacau isia isua pue'i na Racʉnga.
S.PHON: mia tur~ɾupaŋʦu ŋuani ar~ɾaʦakanɨ ʔinia ia neviʦi ŋuani taʦau isia isua pueʔi na r~ɾaʦɨŋa.
S.TRANSL: 他們在那裡結束打獵之後,他們帶著那些狗回到Racʉnga.
When they finished their hunting there, they brought those dogs back to Racʉnga.

S.FORM: nakai sua nikamanumanu tacau isua ia takacicin mavici tamtitu pue'i na rancurunga.
S.PHON: nakai sua nikamanumanu taʦau isua ia takaʦiʦin maviʦi tamtitu pueʔi na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa.
S.FORM: nakai sua nikamanumanu tacau isua ia takacicin mavici tamtitu pue'i na rancurunga.
S.PHON: nakai sua nikamanumanu taʦau isua ia takaʦiʦin maviʦi tamtitu pueʔi na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa.
S.TRANSL: 但是那隻生小孩的母狗獨自帶牠的小狗回到Rancurunga.
However, the dog mother bought her doggies back to Rancurunga herself.

S.FORM: makasua kani popue'i manasʉ 'una makauturu.
S.PHON: makasua kani popueʔi manasɨ ʔuna makautur~ɾu.
S.FORM: makasua kani popue'i manasʉ 'una makauturu.
S.PHON: makasua kani popueʔi manasɨ ʔuna makautur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 據說那樣來來回回有三次
It is said that the dog came and went like that for three times.

S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉcu mamarang Kanakanavu 'unakara vanai neni popue'i sua ah tacau umavici tam tamtituin puei'i na rancurunga.
S.PHON: paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨʦu mamar~ɾaŋ kanakanavu ʔunakar~ɾa vanai neni popueʔi sua ah taʦau umaviʦi tam tamtituin pueiʔi na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa.
S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉcu mamarang Kanakanavu 'unakara vanai neni popue'i sua ah tacau umavici tam tamtituin puei'i na rancurunga.
S.PHON: paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨʦu mamar~ɾaŋ kanakanavu ʔunakar~ɾa vanai neni popueʔi sua ah taʦau umaviʦi tam tamtituin pueiʔi na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa.
S.TRANSL: 當時卡族的老人家就想著狗狗那樣來來回回究竟有什麼原因.
The Kankanavu elders thought why the dog kept bringing her doggies to Rancurung and back again.

S.FORM: makasuacu kan eh ʉncoo mamarang Kanakanavu manasʉ 'e tumatutuuru tikana tekita aravari 'inia.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu kan eh ɨnʦoo mamar~ɾaŋ kanakanavu manasɨ ʔe tumatutuur~ɾu tikana tekita ar~ɾavar~ɾi ʔinia.
S.FORM: makasuacu kan eh ʉncoo mamarang Kanakanavu manasʉ 'e tumatutuuru tikana tekita aravari 'inia.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu kan eh ɨnʦoo mamar~ɾaŋ kanakanavu manasɨ ʔe tumatutuur~ɾu tikana tekita ar~ɾavar~ɾi ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 因此,當時卡那卡那富老人家就想著或許狗狗是在告訴他們,他們該搬家了.

TRANSL.notes: Therefore, elders at that time thought perhaps (those dogs) were telling them that they should move.

S.TRANSL: Therefore, elders at that time thought perhaps were telling them that they should move.

S.FORM: makasuacu kani 'unacu ah upeni kani ah tanasa sasarunai mavici tacau mokusa Rancurunga nia nimon 'inia cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu kani ʔunaʦu ah upeni kani ah tanasa sasar~ɾunai maviʦi taʦau mokusa r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa nia nimon ʔinia ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: makasuacu kani 'unacu ah upeni kani ah tanasa sasarunai mavici tacau mokusa Rancurunga nia nimon 'inia cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu kani ʔunaʦu ah upeni kani ah tanasa sasar~ɾunai maviʦi taʦau mokusa r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa nia nimon ʔinia ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 之後,就有不知道幾位卡族男士帶著狗搬到Rancurunga的地方定居.
Afterwards, many Kanakanavu males moved Rancurunga with their dogs to live there.

S.FORM: nakai pacʉpʉcʉpʉng 'akia nannakʉ uh 'inia ah ka'an mamanʉng siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: nakai paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ ʔakia nannakɨ uh ʔinia ah kaʔan mamanɨŋ siaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: nakai pacʉpʉcʉpʉng 'akia nannakʉ uh 'inia ah ka'an mamanʉng siacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: nakai paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋ ʔakia nannakɨ uh ʔinia ah kaʔan mamanɨŋ siaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 但是又想沒有女人在那裡生活很不方便.
However, they thought without females, they could not have good lives there.

S.FORM: imo pui'i na racurunga umavici nanakʉin mamanu moncu 'inia cumacʉvʉrʉ mumʉkʉ ka'um'uma 'inia.
S.PHON: imo puiʔi na r~ɾaʦur~ɾuŋa umaviʦi nanakɨin mamanu monʦu ʔinia ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ mumɨkɨ kaʔumʔuma ʔinia.
S.FORM: imo pui'i na racurunga umavici nanakʉin mamanu moncu 'inia cumacʉvʉrʉ mumʉkʉ ka'um'uma 'inia.
S.PHON: imo puiʔi na r~ɾaʦur~ɾuŋa umaviʦi nanakɨin mamanu monʦu ʔinia ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ mumɨkɨ kaʔumʔuma ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 所以,他們回到nacʉnga要帶他們的女人和小孩到Racurunga那裏生活、墾地、種田.
SO, they returned to nacʉnga to bring their women and children to Rancurunga to live and farm there.

S.FORM: cumacʉ'ʉra cucumen kanakanavu makai mamanʉng aravari 'inia.
S.PHON: ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa ʦuʦumen kanakanavu makai mamanɨŋ ar~ɾavar~ɾi ʔinia.
S.FORM: cumacʉ'ʉra cucumen kanakanavu makai mamanʉng aravari 'inia.
S.PHON: ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa ʦuʦumen kanakanavu makai mamanɨŋ ar~ɾavar~ɾi ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 其他卡族人看到搬到那裡好像蠻好的.
Other Kanakanavu saw it looked good to move there.

S.FORM: makasuacu kangvang.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu kaŋvaŋ.
S.FORM: makasuacu kangvang.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu kaŋvaŋ.
S.TRANSL: 也都那樣.
They also did that.

S.FORM: moca kangvang 'inia.
S.PHON: moʦa kaŋvaŋ ʔinia.
S.FORM: moca kangvang 'inia.
S.PHON: moʦa kaŋvaŋ ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 也都搬去那裡.
They also moved to that place.

S.FORM: moncu na Rancurunga 'inia.
S.PHON: monʦu na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa ʔinia.
S.FORM: moncu na Rancurunga 'inia.
S.PHON: monʦu na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 就在Rancurunga那個地方.
They lived in Rangurunga.

S.FORM: ka'umo'uma mon 'inia.
S.PHON: kaʔumoʔuma mon ʔinia.
S.FORM: ka'umo'uma mon 'inia.
S.PHON: kaʔumoʔuma mon ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 那裡開墾田園.
They farmed that place.

S.FORM: katatanasa.
S.PHON: katatanasa.
S.FORM: katatanasa.
S.PHON: katatanasa.
S.TRANSL: 蓋房子.
They built houses.

S.FORM: mon 'inia macasU taniara.
S.PHON: mon ʔinia maʦasu taniar~ɾa.
S.FORM: mon 'inia macasU taniara.
S.PHON: mon ʔinia maʦasu taniar~ɾa.
S.TRANSL: 他們在那邊待得很久.
They had lived there for a long time.

S.FORM: isi ia tinimana ʉncoo miana ikita kan kanakanavu ia nimon na rancʉrʉnga aravari na rancurunga 'inia.
S.PHON: isi ia tinimana ɨnʦoo miana ikita kan kanakanavu ia nimon na r~ɾanʦɨr~ɾɨŋa ar~ɾavar~ɾi na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa ʔinia.
S.FORM: isi ia tinimana ʉncoo miana ikita kan kanakanavu ia nimon na rancʉrʉnga aravari na rancurunga 'inia.
S.PHON: isi ia tinimana ɨnʦoo miana ikita kan kanakanavu ia nimon na r~ɾanʦɨr~ɾɨŋa ar~ɾavar~ɾi na r~ɾanʦur~ɾuŋa ʔinia.
S.TRANSL: 這些我所聽到的,據說我們卡族曾經住在Nacʉnga之後搬到 Rancurunga那裡.
These are what I've heard. I've heard that we Kanakanavu once lived in Nacʉnga and then moved to Rancurunga.

S.FORM: nakai mia aratatiacuku manasʉ masʉpatʉnʉcu cenana 'una kanakanavu cau kanakanavu makasi miana kani kanakanavu ia nimon na tainan Anping kisʉʉn masini niarakukun kan ʉncoo.
S.PHON: nakai mia ar~ɾatatiaʦuku manasɨ masɨpatɨnɨʦu ʦenana ʔuna kanakanavu ʦau kanakanavu makasi miana kani kanakanavu ia nimon na tainan anpiŋ kisɨɨn masini niar~ɾakukun kan ɨnʦoo.
S.FORM: nakai mia aratatiacuku manasʉ masʉpatʉnʉcu cenana 'una kanakanavu cau kanakanavu makasi miana kani kanakanavu ia nimon na tainan Anping kisʉʉn masini niarakukun kan ʉncoo.
S.PHON: nakai mia ar~ɾatatiaʦuku manasɨ masɨpatɨnɨʦu ʦenana ʔuna kanakanavu ʦau kanakanavu makasi miana kani kanakanavu ia nimon na tainan anpiŋ kisɨɨn masini niar~ɾakukun kan ɨnʦoo.
S.TRANSL: 但是當我長大之後,大約到了四十歲,有卡族人說以前卡族人曾經住在台南,現在叫做安平的地方,和平埔族一起生活.
However, when I grew up, at about forty years old, I heard some Kanakanavu said once Kanakanavu lived with Pingpu tribes at An-Ping, Tainan.

S.FORM: maica na heto makasuacu mocacaca pui'i makasi na Nansi Meinon Cisan.
S.PHON: maiʦa na heto makasuaʦu moʦaʦaʦa puiʔi makasi na nansi meinon ʦisan.
S.FORM: maica na heto makasuacu mocacaca pui'i makasi na Nansi Meinon Cisan.
S.PHON: maiʦa na heto makasuaʦu moʦaʦaʦa puiʔi makasi na nansi meinon ʦisan.
S.TRANSL: 他們經過屏東,東那樣來來回回地走著往楠西、美濃、旗山的方向移動.
They passed by Pingtong, going back and forth to the direction of Nanxi, Meinong, and Qishan.

S.FORM: mia 'esi na meinon mararuai kani cau.
S.PHON: mia ʔesi na meinon mar~ɾar~ɾuai kani ʦau.
S.FORM: mia 'esi na meinon mararuai kani cau.
S.PHON: mia ʔesi na meinon mar~ɾar~ɾuai kani ʦau.
S.TRANSL: 當在美濃的時候,人們分道揚鑣.
They went separate ways when they were in Meinong.

S.FORM: sua 'una kani cau mutʉkʉtʉkʉ mokusa Uncoo meinon mokusa na riukue mokusa na masini tauien.
S.PHON: sua ʔuna kani ʦau mutɨkɨtɨkɨ mokusa unʦoo meinon mokusa na r~ɾiukue mokusa na masini tauien.
S.FORM: sua 'una kani cau mutʉkʉtʉkʉ mokusa Uncoo meinon mokusa na riukue mokusa na masini tauien.
S.PHON: sua ʔuna kani ʦau mutɨkɨtɨkɨ mokusa unʦoo meinon mokusa na r~ɾiukue mokusa na masini tauien.
S.TRANSL: 據說有的人直走去美濃,去六龜,去桃源.
It is said that some people walked to Meinong, Liugue, and Tauyuen directly.

S.FORM: 'unacu kari makasi sua nguani kani ia nguai sua masini ra'arua misee.
S.PHON: ʔunaʦu kar~ɾi makasi sua ŋuani kani ia ŋuai sua masini r~ɾaʔar~ɾua misee.
S.FORM: 'unacu kari makasi sua nguani kani ia nguai sua masini ra'arua misee.
S.PHON: ʔunaʦu kar~ɾi makasi sua ŋuani kani ia ŋuai sua masini r~ɾaʔar~ɾua misee.
S.TRANSL: 就有這麼一說,他們就是現在的拉阿魯哇族.
There is a word saying that they are Ra'arua that we know now.

S.FORM: 'una kani cau cucumeni mon na cisan mararuai maica na namasia mokusa na masini to'onamita.
S.PHON: ʔuna kani ʦau ʦuʦumeni mon na ʦisan mar~ɾar~ɾuai maiʦa na namasia mokusa na masini toʔonamita.
S.FORM: 'una kani cau cucumeni mon na cisan mararuai maica na namasia mokusa na masini to'onamita.
S.PHON: ʔuna kani ʦau ʦuʦumeni mon na ʦisan mar~ɾar~ɾuai maiʦa na namasia mokusa na masini toʔonamita.
S.TRANSL: 據說還有其他的人在旗山分道走了,經過那瑪夏,到達現在我們的地方.
It is also said that some other people chose another way to go, passing Namasia to where we are living now.

S.FORM: sua isua kani ia nguai sua ikita kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sua isua kani ia ŋuai sua ikita kanakanavu.
S.FORM: sua isua kani ia nguai sua ikita kanakanavu.
S.PHON: sua isua kani ia ŋuai sua ikita kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 那些也就是我們卡那卡那富族人.
Those people are we Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: makasua tinimanamaku.
S.PHON: makasua tinimanamaku.
S.FORM: makasua tinimanamaku.
S.PHON: makasua tinimanamaku.
S.TRANSL: 我所聽到的是那樣.
What I've heard is like that.

S.FORM: nimon kim nimon na nimon kan kanakanavu 'inia macasU taniara.
S.PHON: nimon kim nimon na nimon kan kanakanavu ʔinia maʦasu taniar~ɾa.
S.FORM: nimon kim nimon na nimon kan kanakanavu 'inia macasU taniara.
S.PHON: nimon kim nimon na nimon kan kanakanavu ʔinia maʦasu taniar~ɾa.
S.TRANSL: 據說我們卡族人待在那裡很長的日子.
It is said that we Kanakanavu had stayed there for a very long time.

S.FORM: 'una kanicu niatuumura canumu.
S.PHON: ʔuna kaniʦu niatuumur~ɾa ʦanumu.
S.FORM: 'una kanicu niatuumura canumu.
S.PHON: ʔuna kaniʦu niatuumur~ɾa ʦanumu.
S.TRANSL: 據說有滿出來的水.
It is also said there was overflowing water.

S.FORM: 'una kani vʉrʉnga.
S.PHON: ʔuna kani vɨr~ɾɨŋa.
S.FORM: 'una kani vʉrʉnga.
S.PHON: ʔuna kani vɨr~ɾɨŋa.
S.TRANSL: 據說有鰻魚.
And, an eel.

S.FORM: nimon kan na canumu isua tʉpʉnkee canumu.
S.PHON: nimon kan na ʦanumu isua tɨpɨnkee ʦanumu.
S.FORM: nimon kan na canumu isua tʉpʉnkee canumu.
S.PHON: nimon kan na ʦanumu isua tɨpɨnkee ʦanumu.
S.TRANSL: 據說他在水中堵住了水流.
It is said that it stuck the water.

S.FORM: ara'itumuru canumu.
S.PHON: ar~ɾaʔitumur~ɾu ʦanumu.
S.FORM: ara'itumuru canumu.
S.PHON: ar~ɾaʔitumur~ɾu ʦanumu.
S.TRANSL: 水變多了.
The water became much more.

S.FORM: nipunarU kan kangvang Unco kanakanavu tamna 'umo'uma tanasa.
S.PHON: nipunar~ɾu kan kaŋvaŋ unʦo kanakanavu tamna ʔumoʔuma tanasa.
S.FORM: nipunarU kan kangvang Unco kanakanavu tamna 'umo'uma tanasa.
S.PHON: nipunar~ɾu kan kaŋvaŋ unʦo kanakanavu tamna ʔumoʔuma tanasa.
S.TRANSL: 卡族的耕地以及家都被沖走.
All the fields and houses of Kanakanavu are washed away.

S.FORM: imua manguru kanakanavu mokusa tʉnʉkai ʉnco cimʉrʉ.
S.PHON: imua maŋur~ɾu kanakanavu mokusa tɨnɨkai ɨnʦo ʦimɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: imua manguru kanakanavu mokusa tʉnʉkai ʉnco cimʉrʉ.
S.PHON: imua maŋur~ɾu kanakanavu mokusa tɨnɨkai ɨnʦo ʦimɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 所以卡族人逃到山頂去.
Therefore, Kanakanavu escaped their homes to the top of mountains.

S.FORM: mesua kan ia 'akia kan apuru.
S.PHON: mesua kan ia ʔakia kan apur~ɾu.
S.FORM: mesua kan ia 'akia kan apuru.
S.PHON: mesua kan ia ʔakia kan apur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 據說那個時候沒有火.
At that time, there was no fire.

S.FORM: nakai cumacʉ'ʉra cimʉrʉ cucumen na Tanʉ'ʉcʉ kan.
S.PHON: nakai ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa ʦimɨr~ɾɨ ʦuʦumen na tanɨʔɨʦɨ kan.
S.FORM: nakai cumacʉ'ʉra cimʉrʉ cucumen na Tanʉ'ʉcʉ kan.
S.PHON: nakai ʦumaʦɨʔɨr~ɾa ʦimɨr~ɾɨ ʦuʦumen na tanɨʔɨʦɨ kan.
S.TRANSL: 據說看到在Tanʉ'ʉcʉ的山.
But, they saw another mountain in Tanʉ'ʉcʉ.

S.FORM: 'una kan apuru.
S.PHON: ʔuna kan apur~ɾu.
S.FORM: 'una kan apuru.
S.PHON: ʔuna kan apur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 據說有火.
It is said that there had fire.

S.FORM: mesuakan ia cau mataa marukurakurapʉ ia tavarʉ'ʉ apakarikari akanangkan kari.
S.PHON: mesuakan ia ʦau mataa mar~ɾukur~ɾakur~ɾapɨ ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨ apakar~ɾikar~ɾi akanaŋkan kar~ɾi.
S.FORM: mesuakan ia cau mataa marukurakurapʉ ia tavarʉ'ʉ apakarikari akanangkan kari.
S.PHON: mesuakan ia ʦau mataa mar~ɾukur~ɾakur~ɾapɨ ia tavar~ɾɨʔɨ apakar~ɾikar~ɾi akanaŋkan kar~ɾi.
S.TRANSL: 在那個時候,據說人類和動物可以互相溝通,聽得懂話.
It is said that, at that time, people and animals can understand and communicate with each other.

S.FORM: 'una kan to'urung.
S.PHON: ʔuna kan toʔur~ɾuŋ.
S.FORM: 'una kan to'urung.
S.PHON: ʔuna kan toʔur~ɾuŋ.
S.TRANSL: 有一隻山羌.
There was a muntjac.

S.FORM: makasi sua tia iku mokusa 'inia mokusa tana'ʉcʉ 'inia umara 'apuru misee.
S.PHON: makasi sua tia iku mokusa ʔinia mokusa tanaʔɨʦɨ ʔinia umar~ɾa ʔapur~ɾu misee.
S.FORM: makasi sua tia iku mokusa 'inia mokusa tana'ʉcʉ 'inia umara 'apuru misee.
S.PHON: makasi sua tia iku mokusa ʔinia mokusa tanaʔɨʦɨ ʔinia umar~ɾa ʔapur~ɾu misee.
S.TRANSL: 山羌說:「我要去tana'ʉcʉ拿回火種.」
The muntjac said, 'I will go to tana'ʉcʉ to get the fire.'

S.FORM: nakai 'una kan 'apuru nisʉ'ʉkan na u'ungin nakai makananguru macai canumu musʉpʉrʉ ara'akia canumu.
S.PHON: nakai ʔuna kan ʔapur~ɾu nisɨʔɨkan na uʔuŋin nakai makanaŋur~ɾu maʦai ʦanumu musɨpɨr~ɾɨ ar~ɾaʔakia ʦanumu.
S.FORM: nakai 'una kan 'apuru nisʉ'ʉkan na u'ungin nakai makananguru macai canumu musʉpʉrʉ ara'akia canumu.
S.PHON: nakai ʔuna kan ʔapur~ɾu nisɨʔɨkan na uʔuŋin nakai makanaŋur~ɾu maʦai ʦanumu musɨpɨr~ɾɨ ar~ɾaʔakia ʦanumu.
S.TRANSL: 他把火種放在頭角上游泳過水,結果火種被水浸泡了,火種就滅掉了.
However, the fire was put out by water because the muntjac held the fire on its horns when it was swimming through the water.

S.FORM: marivivini 'una kan tapinange urune kisUUn tapinange isua.
S.PHON: mar~ɾivivini ʔuna kan tapinaŋe ur~ɾune kisuun tapinaŋe isua.
S.FORM: marivivini 'una kan tapinange urune kisUUn tapinange isua.
S.PHON: mar~ɾivivini ʔuna kan tapinaŋe ur~ɾune kisuun tapinaŋe isua.
S.TRANSL: 最後,有隻鳥,被稱作urune
At last, there was a bird, Urune.

S.FORM: tapinange isua ia miana kani nguani nguani tamna ivici ia ka'an masinang.
S.PHON: tapinaŋe isua ia miana kani ŋuani ŋuani tamna iviʦi ia kaʔan masinaŋ.
S.FORM: tapinange isua ia miana kani nguani nguani tamna ivici ia ka'an masinang.
S.PHON: tapinaŋe isua ia miana kani ŋuani ŋuani tamna iviʦi ia kaʔan masinaŋ.
S.TRANSL: 那種鳥,據說以前他們的嘴巴不是紅色的.
it is said that the pecks of those birds were not red.

S.FORM: pasinkankee cau mesua tia nguainkan mokusa na a sipara isa umara 'apuru.
S.PHON: pasinkankee ʦau mesua tia ŋuainkan mokusa na a sipar~ɾa isa umar~ɾa ʔapur~ɾu.
S.FORM: pasinkankee cau mesua tia nguainkan mokusa na a sipara isa umara 'apuru.
S.PHON: pasinkankee ʦau mesua tia ŋuainkan mokusa na a sipar~ɾa isa umar~ɾa ʔapur~ɾu.
S.TRANSL: 鳥對人說由他來去那個對岸拿火種.
The bird said to human beings that he will go to the other side of the river to get the fire back'

S.FORM: hao nevicikankee 'apuru mova kanakanavu nakai si macici curu nguain tamna ivici ia niaramasinang.
S.PHON: hao neviʦikankee ʔapur~ɾu mova kanakanavu nakai si maʦiʦi ʦur~ɾu ŋuain tamna iviʦi ia niar~ɾamasinaŋ.
S.FORM: hao nevicikankee 'apuru mova kanakanavu nakai si macici curu nguain tamna ivici ia niaramasinang.
S.PHON: hao neviʦikankee ʔapur~ɾu mova kanakanavu nakai si maʦiʦi ʦur~ɾu ŋuain tamna iviʦi ia niar~ɾamasinaŋ.
S.TRANSL: 但是因為實在很熱,牠的鳥喙變成紅色.
However, because it is too hot, his mouth became red.

S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉ kanakanavu sua cucuruana mamanʉng urune tapinange isi.
S.PHON: paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨ kanakanavu sua ʦuʦur~ɾuana mamanɨŋ ur~ɾune tapinaŋe isi.
S.FORM: pacʉpʉcʉpʉngʉ kanakanavu sua cucuruana mamanʉng urune tapinange isi.
S.PHON: paʦɨpɨʦɨpɨŋɨ kanakanavu sua ʦuʦur~ɾuana mamanɨŋ ur~ɾune tapinaŋe isi.
S.TRANSL: Kanakanavu想著這個紅嘴黑鵯真正好啊!
Kanakanavu thought this Himalayan Black Bulbul was a really good bird.

S.FORM: imo aranai meesua 'una canimita poisua urune isua.
S.PHON: imo ar~ɾanai meesua ʔuna ʦanimita poisua ur~ɾune isua.
S.FORM: imo aranai meesua 'una canimita poisua urune isua.
S.PHON: imo ar~ɾanai meesua ʔuna ʦanimita poisua ur~ɾune isua.
S.TRANSL: 所以從那時候起,我們有一首關於紅嘴黑鵯的歌.
So from then on, we have a song singing about that Himalayan.Black.Bulbul.

S.FORM: makasuacu mon na mon Kanakanavu na Namasia isi.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu mon na mon kanakanavu na namasia isi.
S.FORM: makasuacu mon na mon Kanakanavu na Namasia isi.
S.PHON: makasuaʦu mon na mon kanakanavu na namasia isi.
S.TRANSL: 那般之後,卡那卡那富族人就住在那瑪夏這裡.
Therefore, Kanakanavu resided there.

S.FORM: iovutucu pakisia iovutucu 'oria iovutucu ripun arivarivarivari kan to'onaamita.
S.PHON: iovutuʦu pakisia iovutuʦu ʔor~ɾia iovutuʦu r~ɾipun ar~ɾivar~ɾivar~ɾivar~ɾi kan toʔonaamita.
S.FORM: iovutucu pakisia iovutucu 'oria iovutucu ripun arivarivarivari kan to'onaamita.
S.PHON: iovutuʦu pakisia iovutuʦu ʔor~ɾia iovutuʦu r~ɾipun ar~ɾivar~ɾivar~ɾivar~ɾi kan toʔonaamita.
S.TRANSL: 閩南人進來 平埔族也來了 日本人來了.據說我們所住的地方一直在移動著.
Hoklo came first, Pingpu people came next, and then Japanese came. It is said that we kept moving our place.

S.FORM: nakai aranai meesua koocu arivari marivari arivari na cucumeni to'ona kanakanavu.
S.PHON: nakai ar~ɾanai meesua kooʦu ar~ɾivar~ɾi mar~ɾivar~ɾi ar~ɾivar~ɾi na ʦuʦumeni toʔona kanakanavu.
S.FORM: nakai aranai meesua koocu arivari marivari arivari na cucumeni to'ona kanakanavu.
S.PHON: nakai ar~ɾanai meesua kooʦu ar~ɾivar~ɾi mar~ɾivar~ɾi ar~ɾivar~ɾi na ʦuʦumeni toʔona kanakanavu.
S.TRANSL: 但是從那時候,我們就沒有再遷移到別的地方了.
Since then, we did not move to any other places.

S.FORM: sua kanakanavu moncu na Namasia isi.
S.PHON: sua kanakanavu monʦu na namasia isi.
S.FORM: sua kanakanavu moncu na Namasia isi.
S.PHON: sua kanakanavu monʦu na namasia isi.
S.TRANSL: 卡那卡那富族人就一直待在那瑪夏這個地方.
Kanakanavu stay at Namasia.

S.FORM: nakai miana 'una cau cucumen noo makari kitana kanakanavu ia makasi sua ikita kan ia kisaa Sumukun taivuran misee.
S.PHON: nakai miana ʔuna ʦau ʦuʦumen noo makar~ɾi kitana kanakanavu ia makasi sua ikita kan ia kisaa sumukun taivur~ɾan misee.
S.FORM: nakai miana 'una cau cucumen noo makari kitana kanakanavu ia makasi sua ikita kan ia kisaa Sumukun taivuran misee.
S.PHON: nakai miana ʔuna ʦau ʦuʦumen noo makar~ɾi kitana kanakanavu ia makasi sua ikita kan ia kisaa sumukun taivur~ɾan misee.
S.TRANSL: 但是,以前有其他人在跟我們卡族人談話時, 叫我們卡族Taivuran,布農族也那樣稱呼我們.
However, in the past, there are some other people called us, as what Bunun called, Taivuran, when they were talking to us.

S.FORM: nakai ka'ankita manman nganai taivuran isi.
S.PHON: nakai kaʔankita manman ŋanai taivur~ɾan isi.
S.FORM: nakai ka'ankita manman nganai taivuran isi.
S.PHON: nakai kaʔankita manman ŋanai taivur~ɾan isi.
S.TRANSL: 但是我們並不喜歡taivuran這個名字
But we do not like taivuran, this name.

S.FORM: makai aranai miana masiraru kita noo tumatuturu cau sua nganaimita ia kanakanavu misee.
S.PHON: makai ar~ɾanai miana masir~ɾar~ɾu kita noo tumatutur~ɾu ʦau sua ŋanaimita ia kanakanavu misee.
S.FORM: makai aranai miana masiraru kita noo tumatuturu cau sua nganaimita ia kanakanavu misee.
S.PHON: makai ar~ɾanai miana masir~ɾar~ɾu kita noo tumatutur~ɾu ʦau sua ŋanaimita ia kanakanavu misee.
S.TRANSL: 從以前開始,我們在告訴別人時,我們都自稱為卡那卡那富族.
Since a long time ago, when we were talking to other people, we always called ourselves Kanakanavu.

S.FORM: makasuacu nimon manasu nimoncukita na namasia isi upeen kusai?
S.PHON: makasuaʦu nimon manasu nimonʦukita na namasia isi upeen kusai?
S.FORM: makasuacu nimon manasu nimoncukita na namasia isi upeen kusai?
S.PHON: makasuaʦu nimon manasu nimonʦukita na namasia isi upeen kusai?
S.TRANSL: 那樣,我們卡那卡那富族待在那瑪夏這裡究竟有多久了?
Then, how long have we Kanakanavu lived here?

S.FORM: manasʉ 'una usʉpatʉ nʉmʉ nokai 'una urima nʉmʉ nimonkita na namasia isi cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: manasɨ ʔuna usɨpatɨ nɨmɨ nokai ʔuna ur~ɾima nɨmɨ nimonkita na namasia isi ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.FORM: manasʉ 'una usʉpatʉ nʉmʉ nokai 'una urima nʉmʉ nimonkita na namasia isi cumacʉvʉrʉ.
S.PHON: manasɨ ʔuna usɨpatɨ nɨmɨ nokai ʔuna ur~ɾima nɨmɨ nimonkita na namasia isi ʦumaʦɨvɨr~ɾɨ.
S.TRANSL: 我們待在那瑪夏這裡生長大概有四百或五百年了.
Perhaps We have lived in Namasia and grew up there for four hundred or five hundred years.

S.FORM: makasia sua sua tia tuturuamaku sosomanpe.
S.PHON: makasia sua sua tia tutur~ɾuamaku sosomanpe.
S.FORM: makasia sua sua tia tuturuamaku sosomanpe.
S.PHON: makasia sua sua tia tutur~ɾuamaku sosomanpe.
S.TRANSL: 我所要講的就是這樣.謝謝.
This is all I want to tell you. Thank you.
